content.claim: Short-term test-score gains in selective tracks do not translate to reliable long-run tertiary-education or labor-market outcome improvements; the value-added advantage attenuates by adulthood.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Equity and social mobility lens
limitations: Tertiary and labor-market effects may depend on unmeasured post-secondary educational pathways and job-market timing
Attenuation could reflect changed labor-market demand rather than ineffectiveness of track selection per se
Limited sample size and timeframe for long-run follow-up in some studies
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
